revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]剣野町2-18 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] |社格 = 旧県社・式内社(小) |創建 = [[741年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。 ==…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]剣野町2-18 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] |社格 = 旧県社・式内社(小) |創建 = [[741年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] ; 配祀 * [[孝霊天皇|大日本根子彦太瓊命]] * [[吉備津彦命]]
== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[1973年]]、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みしましんしや}} [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:柏崎市の歴史‎]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

[[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=柏崎市}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]剣野町2-18 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[式内社]](小) |創建 = [[741年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=三島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] ; 配祀 * [[孝霊天皇|大日本根子彦太瓊命]] * [[吉備津彦命]]
== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[1973年]]、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

{{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=柏崎市}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]剣野町2-18 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[式内社]](小) |創建 = [[741年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=三島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] ; 配祀 * [[孝霊天皇|大日本根子彦太瓊命]] * [[吉備津彦命]]
== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[1973年]]、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 三島神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=柏崎市}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]剣野町2-18 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[式内社]](小) |創建 = [[741年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=三島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[柏崎市]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] ; 配祀 * [[孝霊天皇|大日本根子彦太瓊命]] * [[吉備津彦命]]
== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[1973年]]、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みしましんしや}} [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:柏崎市の建築物]] [[Category:柏崎市の歴史]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]
==End Japanese==

== 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] ; 配祀 * [[孝霊天皇|大日本根子彦太瓊命]] * [[吉備津彦命]]
== 歴史 == [[927年]]に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]式内社の、[[越後国]]三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title=[[特選神名牒]]|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。[[伊予国]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたもので、創建は[[741年]]と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[宇佐美氏]]を始め、その後の[[長尾氏#越後長尾氏|越後長尾氏]]（上杉氏）や[[高田藩]]歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。[[特選神名牒]]に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。
[[1873年]]県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
[[1973年]]、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:柏崎市の建築物]] [[Category:柏崎市の歴史]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

}}
'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|新潟県|Q132705}}{{jalink|柏崎市|Q633983}}にある{{jalink|三島・大山祇信仰|Q127948103}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

== 祭神 ==
; 主祭神
* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
; 配祀
* {{jalink|孝霊天皇|Q313041|大日本根子彦太瓊命}}
* {{jalink|吉備津彦命|Q65318}}

== 歴史 ==
{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}式内社の、{{jalink|越後国|Q666514}}三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。{{jalink|伊予国|Q907829}}{{jalink|大三島|Q9009869}}の{{jalink|大山祇神社|Q703904}}から勧請されたもので、創建は{{jalink|741年|Q23597}}と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|宇佐美氏|Q24860892}}を始め、その後の{{jalink|長尾氏#越後長尾氏|Q1655561|越後長尾氏}}（上杉氏）や{{jalink|高田藩|Q839422}}歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

== 出典 ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

{{shinto-stub}}

==End Japanese==

'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|新潟県|Q132705}}{{jalink|柏崎市|Q633983}}にある{{jalink|三島・大山祇信仰|Q127948103}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 祭神 ===
; 主祭神
* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
; 配祀
* {{jalink|孝霊天皇|Q313041|大日本根子彦太瓊命}}
* {{jalink|吉備津彦命|Q65318}}

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}式内社の、{{jalink|越後国|Q666514}}三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。{{jalink|伊予国|Q907829}}{{jalink|大三島|Q9009869}}の{{jalink|大山祇神社|Q703904}}から勧請されたもので、創建は{{jalink|741年|Q23597}}と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|宇佐美氏|Q24860892}}を始め、その後の{{jalink|長尾氏#越後長尾氏|Q1655561|越後長尾氏}}（上杉氏）や{{jalink|高田藩|Q839422}}歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

=== 出典 ===

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}式内社の、{{jalink|越後国|Q666514}}三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。{{jalink|伊予国|Q907829}}{{jalink|大三島|Q9009869}}の{{jalink|大山祇神社|Q703904}}から勧請されたもので、創建は{{jalink|741年|Q23597}}と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|宇佐美氏|Q24860892}}を始め、その後の{{jalink|長尾氏#越後長尾氏|Q1655561|越後長尾氏}}（上杉氏）や{{jalink|高田藩|Q839422}}歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

=== 出典 ===

{{Reflist}}

{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みしましんしや}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''三島神社'''（みしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|新潟県|Q132705}}{{jalink|柏崎市|Q633983}}にある{{jalink|三島・大山祇信仰|Q127948103}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 祭神 ===
; 主祭神
* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
; 配祀
* {{jalink|孝霊天皇|Q313041|大日本根子彦太瓊命}}
* {{jalink|吉備津彦命|Q65318}}

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}式内社の、{{jalink|越後国|Q666514}}三嶋郡三嶋神社に比定される神社である<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>。{{jalink|伊予国|Q907829}}{{jalink|大三島|Q9009869}}の{{jalink|大山祇神社|Q703904}}から勧請されたもので、創建は{{jalink|741年|Q23597}}と伝えられる<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|宇佐美氏|Q24860892}}を始め、その後の{{jalink|長尾氏#越後長尾氏|Q1655561|越後長尾氏}}（上杉氏）や{{jalink|高田藩|Q839422}}歴代藩主の崇敬を受けた<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（柏崎市）</ref>。{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}に拠れば、元々石地駅の東五里、三島谷村にあったが、当地に移したものという。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}県社となる。ただしその後の火災により、当時の面影はない<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。現在の社殿は、1931年に再建されたものである<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}、市指定文化財（記念物・史跡）に指定された<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>。

=== 出典 ===

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103}} {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwazaki|Q633983}}, {{jalink|Niigata Prefecture|Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{jalink|Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{jalink|Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904}} on {{jalink|Ōmishima|Q9009869}} in {{jalink|Iyo Province|Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{jalink|741|Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{jalink|Usami clan|Q24860892}}, then from the {{jalink|Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{jalink|Takada Domain|Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

According to the {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103}} {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwazaki|Q633983}}, {{jalink|Niigata Prefecture|Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{jalink|Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{jalink|Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904}} on {{jalink|Ōmishima|Q9009869}} in {{jalink|Iyo Province|Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{jalink|741|Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{jalink|Usami clan|Q24860892}}, then from the {{jalink|Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{jalink|Takada Domain|Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103}} {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwazaki|Q633983}}, {{jalink|Niigata Prefecture|Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{jalink|Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{jalink|Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904}} on {{jalink|Ōmishima|Q9009869}} in {{jalink|Iyo Province|Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{jalink|741|Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{jalink|Usami clan|Q24860892}}, then from the {{jalink|Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{jalink|Takada Domain|Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103}} {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwazaki|Q633983}}, {{jalink|Niigata Prefecture|Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{jalink|Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{jalink|Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904}} on {{jalink|Ōmishima|Q9009869}} in {{jalink|Iyo Province|Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{jalink|741|Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{jalink|Usami clan|Q24860892}}, then from the {{jalink|Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{jalink|Takada Domain|Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103|WD=Q127948103}} {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwazaki|Q633983|WD=Q633983}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|lt=Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|WD=Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103|WD=Q127948103}} {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwazaki|Q633983|WD=Q633983}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|lt=Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|WD=Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

'''Mishima Shrine''' (みしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Mishima and Ōyamazumi worship|Q127948103|WD=Q127948103}} {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwazaki|Q633983|WD=Q633983}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}.

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
; Associated deities
* {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|lt=Ōyamato Nekohikofutoni no Mikoto}}
* {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|WD=Q65318}}

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

=== History ===
This shrine is identified with Mishima Shrine in Mishima District of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|WD=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}, listed as a Shikinaisha in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=教部省|author=|year=1925|title={{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}|publisher=磯部甲陽堂|page=|isbn=|ref=}}</ref>. It was invited from {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Ōmishima|Q9009869|WD=Q9009869|1=Ōmishima Island}} in {{ill|lt=Iyo Province|Q907829|WD=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}}, and is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=741|Q23597|WD=Q23597|1=741}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

It received devotion from the {{ill|lt=Usami clan|Q24860892|WD=Q24860892|1=Usami clan}}, then from the {{ill|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|lt=Echigo Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}} (Uesugi clan), and successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|WD=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}}<ref name=柏崎市三島神社>[https://www.city.kashiwazaki.lg.jp/soshikiichiran/sangyoshinkobu/shogyokankoka/kankosinko/23/25/3/3627.html 柏崎市名所案内「三島神社(みしまじんじゃ)」]（Kashiwazaki City）</ref>. According to the {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, it was originally in Mishimatani village five ri east of Ishiji Station, and later moved to the current site.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} it became a prefectural shrine. However, due to later fire, it no longer retains the former appearance<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>. The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1931<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}}, it was designated a city cultural property (monument, historic site)<ref name=柏崎市三島神社/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishima Shrine'''|三島神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].
